title: Stenandrium humile
taxonomy: Stenandrium humile is classified within the family Acanthaceae. This family comprises flowering plants commonly known as acanthus or thistle family. Stenandrium humile is also classified within the genus Stenandrium, a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. The genus Stenandrium is part of the larger order Lamiales, which includes plants with bilaterally symmetrical flowers. The classification of Stenandrium humile reflects its evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.
uses: Stenandrium humile is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its reported antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Its roots are often employed to alleviate fever and other symptoms of colds and flu. The plant's leaves are also utilized to treat digestive issues and as a remedy for sore throats. Additionally, Stenandrium humile is believed to possess antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal applications and benefits.

intro: Stenandrium humile, also known as Small Calathea or Small Stenandrium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Stenandrium humile include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Stenandrium humile is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also sometimes used in the production of tinctures and extracts.
